channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum threads
channel.description: Threads in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: Seeking my first critique!
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article
Genre : Emotional

Elevator Pitch: A small cube of human flesh was 'born' in an abandoned maternity ward. It exudes emotional control over those near it, affecting one Foundation agent quite radically.

Central Narrative: Agent Vega, an operative on the scene during initial containment, becomes obsessed with taking care of the cube, and perceives it as a human baby, and as his own biological son.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The cube feeds on being cared for like a baby, and is not necessarily malicious on its own. The cube isn't sentient and the actions of weeping and crying like a child are just biological impulses it has to survive. Ultimately, the SCP has no feelings and just wants to live, on the very base level that simple mammals do.

Additional Notes: I don't have anything of particular worry but I am excited for folks to take a look! I have my sandbox ready to go whenever someone wants to take a look.

item.title: [SEEKING GREENLIGHTS] for SCP idea
item.description: A pokémon video game where every NPC is self aware, and the ethical dilema of dealing with their society
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: I'm not sure

Page Layout: A normal article, with several addendums

Elevator Pitch: The inhabitants of the world split into two factions and are about to get into a war. The player could stop it, since being the protagonist of the game the inhabitants of it treat him like a celebrity.

This presents a dilema to the Foundation: Should they interviene? If the people inside of the game are to be considered human, should the Foundation policy of political neutrality apply?

Central Narrative: A cartridge of Pokémon HeartGold where every NPC is sentient and self-aware. Every inhabitant of the world lives a life on their own, without knowing they are inside of a video game. The player cannot really communicate with them (since that's not an option in Pokémon games), but the Foundation can monitor their society by talking to the NPCs, whose dialog changes regularly.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I think a mini Pokémon world living inside a cartridge is a pretty good concept which hasn't been tried before.

Additional Notes: I feel the article lacks a central narrative to tie it all together. I may need help with that.

item.title: SCP Concept: I do not have a body
item.description: An entity akin to a ghost. Whenever it takes or is given a body (according to its multiple definitions in a certain dictionary), that body ceases to exist.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Page Layout (Optional): Because of reasons specified in further sections, the article would be written solely in headings, images, and footnotes; that is to say, no body text.

Elevator Pitch: A cloud of massless, blue gas, around the size of a yoga ball, with two small discs of lighter color that mimic the appearance of cartoon-like eyes. It can move approximately walking speed in any direction, hear, understand, and speak English, and can “see” through its “eyes.” Whenever it possesses something (whether by taking it or by being given it, whether it is aware or not) that is considered to be a “body” by an unspecified dictionary (Merriam-Webster), that thing disappears.

Central Narrative: This SCP claims to have originally been a human that was “cursed.” It acts benevolently towards humans, and it voluntarily turned itself in to the Foundation. It was found that the SCP would not willingly take the body of a human (living or dead), and would have to be forcefully “given” it for the body to disappear (i.e. “you can have this human” would not work, but “this human is yours” would). Further, if someone said “this body now belongs to the SCP,” even if the SCP was out of earshot and unaware of the body’s existence, the body would disappear. The dictionary-bound nature of the SCP was then discovered. One test found that when it was given the “flavour of this wine,” the wine became tasteless. Partway through testing, the SCP began to feel lethargic. It was found that the dictionary’s council was considering adding an additional definition to its page that would include the SCP. This was swiftly prevented, but it made the Foundation aware of a killswitch. After sufficient testing was conducted, the containment procedure was decided; a small orbital craft with 2-way audio connection. If the signal is disrupted for too long of a time, it is to be assumed it has breached containment and will be terminated immediately. Further, its existence is kept to be top-secret, and all involved personnel without sufficient authorization have been administered amnestic.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Unlike most overpowered villains, it has interesting limitations and abilities. Since this SCP is able to cause most things to disappear, it is incredibly dangerous. However, this same ability makes it very powerful if controlled by the foundation. It can also only to be used as a last-ditch effort, though. For example, the unexplained disappearance of SCP-682 would definitely cause some alarm and raise some questions. Thus, the Foundation would be inclined to keep it alive, but only as a last resort.

item.title: The bad omen man
item.description: Keep him entertained, or else
item.content:encoded:
Greenlight: Possibly

Page type: Scp Article

Genre: Fantasy

Elevator pitch: The bad omen man is an unfortunate anomaly that seems to break everything around him against his will. He is kind and witty, but with a short temper.

Central Narrative: The bad omen man is a tall black humanoid, with their facial structure resembling a bat. When bored the structures around them accelerate deterioration by about 10 fold, resulting in poor foundation and containment breaches. When upset, things tend to happen like breaches with no logic of how it could have happened, sudden personnel death, and in one case, the detonation of the internals of a generator causing a site-wide blackout and breaches. This is not restricted to any radius around him, happening all over the world, and even on other planets, and close realities. Fortunately, if given time, he will bond with personnel. He enjoys video games, board games, and honestly is a very polite and kind individual, despite his short temper.

Hook/Attention getter: Entertainment is a good thing, but sometimes it's the most important thing.

item.title: SCP-5547
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -11.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: end of the world, just you and me -Tale Idea
item.description: What happens when 2 people survive the end
item.content:encoded:
Warning, there might be potentially triggering content here. I'm an amateur writer so I don't know if I can handle these topics very well yet.

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: Tale

Genre (Optional): Drama/Emotional

Page Layout (Optional): Typical story structure (maybe a choose your own adventure short?)

Elevator Pitch: end of the world scenario, 2 people survive, one of them decides that living in an empty world of just pain isn't worth it and decides to end their own life. The second person after living with the other for so long is crushed by this and either goes to recover from this trauma or is impacted by this so heavily that she loses the will to go on.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: GD-Class Gestalt Dissolution Scenario occurs

90% of the population vulnerable without a specific immunity gene is turned into eldritch primal hunters, extremely dangerous with varying anomalous effects ranging from instantly causing simulated 3rd degree burns to resurrecting the dead as mindless slaves. All of which with diminished intelligence and fight each other regularly in tribe-like primitive collections, leading to them becoming sparse in population without method of copulating. Self destructive in nature, although powerful.

The story is set around 2 people, a man and woman. They are happy to have each other around in this apocalypse, they survive for months through continuous hardship fighting/avoiding these beings. But the man is becoming more and more tired, he figures that life will never become better, every second of every single day at every inconvenience he just wants it to be over. To him, this could be a tempory problem, but what if it never gets better? What is the point of living through this? Everyday involves pain with no reward in sight. The man decides to give himself to one of the hunters right in front of the woman.

so there's 2 ways this ends, tell me which one sounds better:
a) the woman is depressed for a while but then recovers stronger from the death of her friend, moving past it and continues with the hope of a better future

b) the woman is completely broken by the death of her friend and falls into a depression adopting the hopelessness of the man and is mauled by the hunters

I also have an alternate setting: humanity is obliterated, there is barely anyone still living. The man and woman are alone and that sense of loneliness without hope of a better future. The man, living with depression since he was very young only has it amplified in this bleak setting and he dies, not from monsters but by his own volition.

Which leaves the woman alone, without anyone left alive. The only person who kept her company dies from something completely out of her control.
a) she keeps going and finds someone else
b) she never finds anyone else and continues surviving by herself in a castaway situation in the company of a backpack with a face drawn on it.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The end of the world scenario in of itself as a starting point and the interesting relationship between these two in this new world would be a good way to introduce the story. Their daily lives and their battles and the like, before arriving at the climax to make it impactful.

thank you for reviewing my idea! I know it has a lot of problems but I'm happy to hear feedback.


Got it, thanks zyn mate, I'll keep in mind those mobile users next time

item.title: SCP Idea: the music box
item.description: This is another idea I’ve created. Hopefully it is good enough
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes
Page Type: Article
Genre: Horror
Page Layout:
+ show block
– hide block

Lyrics (of the song SCP-XXXX plays)
Item Number, Object Class
Containment Procedures
Description
Exploration Logs
Any addendums needed

Elevator Pitch: It is a music box made of painted blue brass with a handle also made of brass. Opening the music box shows a figurine of a young female student. The mechanism is the reverse of a non-anomalous music box, the handle needs wound for the music not to play. Any sentient being that hears the song will go into an REM sleep and dream of a school, in which they are hunted by a humanoid entity

Narrative:
D-Class find a cassette tape players in the locker they wake up by
Throughout the school, cassette tapes are found, revealing what happened to the school
Last exploration, D-Class finds files on a dangerous student before being killed.
(Planned theme: How certain people are left behind - will be revealed through tapes and files)

Hook: The weird setup for this SCP. The idea is for the reader to ask questions to encourage them to read further.

Additional: The “narrative through exploration logs” is what 087 and I’m afraid this will just be a 087 ripoff.

item.title: Requesting Greenlight for SCP (Your Chance to Win)
item.description: A gameshow host with a twist.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Semi-horror

Page Layout (Optional): SCP Class (Euclid), Containment Protocol, Description, (Possibly Picture)

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a radio tower built somewhere around the 1980s around Ballard, Utah. It has not been fed power since its abandonment somewhere between 1985 and 1990. The tower broadcasts a radio signal which is still able to be picked up by radio receivers approximately six miles in the surrounding area.

Central Narrative: Every three to four days, SCP-XXXX broadcasts a signal in which a excited voice is heard announcing something (email, last few credit card numbers, social security number, home address, license etc.) to identify a seemingly random individual. Other than these occurances, only static can be heard when tuned to the channel. (This is bizarre due to the lack of power being sent to the tower)

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Within the least recorded timeframe of a few hours to the most of roughly a year, an entity (deemed SCP-XXXX-2) visits the individual previously announced on the radio transmission. This entity is dressed in a formal attire with a black suit, pants, and shoes with a white undershirt and purple tie. SCP-XXXX-2 tells the person that he is from a reward group and that the person has been chosen to have a chance at winning "glamorous prizes" and requests to sit down with the individual. In the case that the subject accepts, SCP-XXXX-2 expresses apparent gratitude and sits down with them. Once seated, he asks the subject questions ranging from trivia, to multiple choice. If the subject answers correctly, SCP-XXXX-2 leaves their sight and disappears. The following day, the subject receives gifts such as new kitchen equipment, cars, furniture, decor or gift cards. In the case that the subject declines, SCP-XXXX-2 shows frustrating, discomfort and disdain towards the subject. As the same as the previous case, leaves the subject's sight and disappears. The exception to this case is that immediately (30 seconds) to later (days to weeks) something damaging happens to the subject's house. (chandeliers coming loose and falling, to kidnapping/home invasion)

Additional Notes: Please rate the professionality of the article and the overall idea. Thank you.

item.title: SCP-Idea: Museum Of Reminiscence
item.description: A Museum invades your deepest memories and brings them to light!
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greelight: Yes
Page Type: SCP Article
Genre: Drama
Page Layout: Standard SCP Article

Elevator Pinch: Anomalitic properties happen when an individual remains inside the Museum for more than 30m, then the individual perceives the reality changing until the interior of the Museum is completely new, at this stage the reality inside the Museum is completely connected with some memory of the individual, normally the memory is deep and has great importance for the individual. It is important to say that if there is more than one person inside the Museum, the anomaly will manifest a memory that connects all individuals in the Museum. If by chance there is no connection between all individuals then the anomaly will not happen.

Central Narrative: the story involves the researcher who is doing the article as he will participate in the story as well. The researcher in question has a troubled past and the experiment in which he is the guinea pig itself will be the most important part of the SCP

Attention-Grabber: Hmmm I think that is the first problem I have. I dunno if I have really a unique thing with my SCP. This is my first attempt anyway so will put my Additional Notes here because if anyone have an Idea of some "Special Flavor" to put in my SCP than I would be thankful.

Anyway that is my SCP Idea. Quite a simple one if you ask me.

item.title: SCP-4273
item.content:encoded: Images are public domain, will provide more detailed info tonight. Thanks for reading.

item.title: Translation
item.description: How do i translate an article?
item.content:encoded: I thought about translating an article from French to English (an original SCP in French). If I do translate the article and put it in the sandbox, I am allowed to directly post it on the main site? Or should I seek feedback first (if so, here or on the -FR wiki)?

item.title: SCP-5134
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, but there isn't really that much there that makes it an SCP. I believe there is a list of anomalous items that don't have enough properties to really be a full-blown SCP article. It could be pretty good over there. I like the idea, but the execution could use some touching-up.

I also feel like the containment procedures are extremely overdone. If it can be safely kept in a box, the foundation will put it in a box. or, more accurately, a secured locker. If the pedestal isn't necessary for containment, it shouldn't be there. If it is, explain why. The clinical tone could use quite a bit of work too.

If you really want this to be an SCP, I recommend working on it to try to make it better for being used for storytelling purposes.

I've also realized that this SCP's clearance requirements are way too high given it's anomalous abilities. The O-5 have much bigger fish to fry than someone wanting a book to have their name in it. And level-4 clearance requirements are also too high. Level 4-clearance is considered Top-Secret. This is something that you really don't want the most important people in the foundation touching, as it gives their enemies a nice little list of everyone important in the foundation and their names.

item.title: SCP Idea: Monster Under the Bed (Seeking Greenlight)
item.description: What if the monster under your bed was actually nice?
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Object class Safe
  • The SCP is a mattress
  • Whenever someone lays down on the mattress, a monster appears and tells a bedtime story

Central Narrative: The mattress was brought to the attention of the Foundation when a married couple who had just bought it reported seeing a monster emerge from under their young daughter's bed after she had mentioned it and insisted on showing them. After being taken into Foundation custody and having its properties examined, it was eventually given to a researcher living on-site to treat their insomnia.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Whenever a person lies down on the mattress, a large humanoid entity resembling a carnivorous mammal and covered in thick fur will suddenly appear. If the mattress is placed on a bed frame, the entity will crawl out from underneath the bed, and if the mattress is placed on the floor, the entity will appear from any concealed space nearby, such as inside a closet. If there are no concealed areas in close proximity to the mattress, the entity will not appear. After the entity appears, it will gently sit down on the side of the mattress and begin to tell the person a bedtime story, typically lasting approximately ten minutes. If the entity is allowed to finish its story, it will then tuck the person in if blankets are present, wish them good night, and return to where it appeared from, after which it will disappear. The person on the mattress will then fall asleep, remaining unconscious for a full eight hours, even if they have a condition that hinders sleep such as insomnia, unless disturbed by outside stimuli sufficient to awaken them, such as an alarm or someone shaking them. If the person is frightened by their appearance and attempts to leave the bed, the entity will become saddened and leave, and the person will remain awake. Those who listen to the entity's full story and sleep on the mattress wake up feeling well-rested, and they typically report pleasant dreams.

Additional Notes: If anyone has feedback on how I could improve the idea, I'd appreciate being let known. I also want to make sure that this idea isn't too similar to an existing SCP, so please let me know if the idea has already been taken.

item.title: The Broken toys
item.description: The Broken toys are a group of entities that hunt bad children and turn them into parts for more toys, to get more bad children.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight Yes

Page Type SCP Article

Genre (optional):Horror

Elevator Pitch: The broken toys are a group of entities that resemble beaten-up old toys with extra or missing limbs and these toys turn naughty or bad children into plastic pieces to make more toys to help with getting rid of more bad children.

Central Narrative A family of one father, mother, and two kids are opening gifts on Christmas day,
one of those kids was very bad that year, and he received a very odd-looking deformed toy. later that day the family went looking for their child and could only find the toy's packaging and a note saying thanks for the donation.

item.title: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh this has been a bit stressful writing it to make sure I didn't downgrade the characters or story! AM sorry for the wait!

Anyways on to the thanks! I would like to thank Cydhra, Mooagain, Vivarium, Mars, and Quadraginta for the critique and willingness to help me make sure this is as good as I can do with my… lack of writing techniques. I also wanna thank MalyceGraves and plaidypus for creating both the world of La Rue Macabre and the Mekhanics cause it is a lot of fun to write for the world.

And as always thank you for reading this, reader! I'm just happy to know your reading this.

(PS I am very sorry for the stiff prose, my skills are still terrible at writing in tale form)

item.title: (Seeking green lights) Ever wonder where your things went?
item.description: [thread locked, repeat]
item.content:encoded:
Seeking green lights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Class: Euclid

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a female elf who has the ability to summon items from anywhere in the multi verse. However even though it sounds like X-men syndrome, she can only summon items that she has a complete understanding of how they are made and/or works.
For example she knows how a T-shirt is made so she can summon one, and she knows how a lightbulb is made and works so she can make one. She has a photographic memory that helps her summon objects. The objects come through a multi-universal portal that appears and disappears within the space of 0.2 seconds.
She has a standard - simple humanoid containment cell and has been allowed access to level 01 areas of the facility she is housed in. She is very cooperative with foundation staff, seemingly understanding of why she is locked up. However she has been declared as a threat because the staff cannot say if she is lying or not. Multiple researchers have agreed that she must be monitored and her cell is to be fully checked every two days as well as all actions done by this SCP. The foundation agrees that she is a dangerous scp and should be heavily monitored

Hook/Attention-Grabber: She can summon life forms that she knows the biology of, however no summond-life form has made it out of the portal in one piece, sometimes it may be missing a limb or patch of fur, or it may just be a bloody mess.

She was discovered 1 year ago when foundation staff noticed a sudden surge in gamma radiation in the area. After having onsite personnel in the area for a day, a portal opened up reveling the SCP and an unknown entity behind it. The unknown entity pushed SCP through and closed the portal behind her. She then quickly surrend willingly.
She came from a universe where her race was being hunted and killed. Her race was able to summon items and track each other via a form of telepathy. The limits of this are unknown but the SCP claims that her race frequently traveled the multi verse aided by a machine they used. Researchers have agreed that a small group of mtf should be close by and ready to terminate the SCP if she shows any signs of hostility.

item.title: Containment question
item.content:encoded: So Im writing an SCP that has base requirements of supplies (food) to survive… would that information go in the containment section of the article?

item.title: What about a Canadien Site?
item.content:encoded: Hey! So I have seen that there's a Site for (almost) all languages, and I wonder if a country such as Canada or the United Kingdom could have their own Site or a type of SCP/Goi/Canon for them. I know that the other Sites are basically just translation, but it could make people from America wanna see Goi Canadien and England, and after that, they could be curious to see other translations of Sites such as Japan, China, France, German, Russian, Korean Ect.

I've been sitting on this draft for months now, so I think it's time I put this up.

item.title: Guitar Jail (Seeking Greenlight)
item.description: [thread locked, repeat]
item.content:encoded:
A caucasian man in his late 20's/early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of an acoustic guitar.

Thread summary moved to the main post as the thread is now locked.

A caucasian man in his late 20's/ early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of a guitar.

The man wears an orange prisoner outfit and has no recollection of who he is or how he ended up there. He appears to be missing the thumb, index finger and pinky on his right hand. The man doesn't appear to require any sustenance and cannot reach out of the guitar due to an invisible barrier, although objects can pass through this barrier.

The jail cell appears to be made out of stone and concrete and can be observed through the soundhole of the guitar. There is a light bulb hanging from the centre of the room that keeps the room lit at all times and a bed attached to the wall. Any attempts to damage the cell from outside of the guitar have all failed and resulted in the man feeling great pain throughout his body.

The guitar is a typical 6 string acoustic guitar. The guitar seems to be indestructible. There is a logo and name of the non-existent brand "Slaves to the Sound" on the back of the guitar. Any movements the guitar makes do not appear to effect the jail cell or man. When playing the guitar, the man instinctively and perfectly mimics the playing of the guitar. The sounds produced by the guitar do not possess any anomalous properties.

item.title: Seeking Greenlight: Cloning a Thaumiel Human?
item.description: What happened when the foundation replace D-Class
item.content:encoded:
What happened when the foundation replace D-Class with clones of a Thaumiel SCP?

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP describes both the phenomenal surrounding the clones of the Thaumiel human and the Thaumiel human himself. That cloning a Thaumiel entity produced an entirely different effect from what the O5 expected

Central Narrative:

+ show block
– hide block

The story will be presented through lists of testing logs with D-classes which all ended with failure and breaches. With a message from O5-1 to other O5 debating to change the "execution of the project".

Then the story focus on a containment specialist (later reveal to be the clone of the thaumiel guy) who sent a message to the O5, saying that every SCPs he worked on has become more aggressive/harder to contain. His message is denied.

The Doctor creates an SCP draft, describing a phenomenon in which SCPs are now becoming harder to contain etc. The draft was denied.

This is followed by his journal, saying that he think the O5 must have been using some anomalies to their own personal gains, creating shifts in reality(which might be the reason why SCPs are getting more dangerous etc.). His evident is supported by the fact that all his concern/messages are denied. He wants to expose them.

A database for project "kamino" (a reference to the Clone from Star-Wars) has an unauthorized access by this doctor.

He is terminated by a mematic agent.

A message from O5-1 to the O5, saying that the project must be stopped, judging from their observation on the clones and their effect on the SCPs. Even worse, the doctor journal has been posted, causing mass distrust amongst personnel towards the O5.

Then finally a message from O5 to all personnel, explaining everything concerning the journal, the failed containment of many SCPs and finally publishing the document for the Thaumiel SCP, whose presence "calms" organic beings around a small radius.


Hook/Attention-Grabber:
+ show block
– hide block

Most of the story would make the reader think that the SCP is a phenomenon or reality bending entity effecting "the containment of SCPs", judging from the failed testing logs and reports of more frequent breach in containment. While the document hinted that all the D-Class are "the same person", people might not have spotted it until it is revealed at the end with the documentation for the Thaumiel SCP.


Additional Notes:

item.title: [Seeking Tips and Possibly, Greenlight] an old arcade console
item.description: Arcade game that seems to have an Influence
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:
An old Arcade Gaming Console, entitled "A Devil's Adventure" which seems to make anyone who play it slowly changes behavior until he/she becomes violent.
It is also noted that after being played, the console just turns off for the rest of the day, turning back on the next day.

Central Narrative:
Discovered in an old, abandoned building, two homeless beggars went to look for shelter one rainy night. Upon finding the building, they quickly went inside due to the heavy rains. They then began searching the place up, finding the scp itself, which surprisingly, was still working. One of them then tried it out. But after playing the game, the scp just turned off. Since then, the beggar is observed being rude, making threats and all that. But the other one dismissed it and slept for the night.
On the following morning, the other beggar was awaken and was invited by unknown people which was turned out to be foundation personnel. As it turns out that the beggar who played the scp was arrested by the police for beating up a rival homeless beggar, who upon interrogation by the police, said some gibberish things, tipping of a foundation agent who was embedded at the station, for the existence of the scp . The agent reported it to his superiors, who in turn authorized an investigation whether there was an anomaly involved in it.
Later testings by d class personnel and analysis would reveal that this was an scp, and that this has to be contained. Later investigation would trace the scp back to a disastrous fire that occurred at a cult's hideout in the 80s
Killing off the entire occupants, destroying everything, except the scp itself.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:
A simple idea of how one thing can change and turn someone, 'evilish'.

Additional Notes:
This is an idea which I salvaged, and revised from it being abandoned a year ago due to personal reasons. I did my best to make this idea into it's best. So feel free to drop any feedback. Suggestions on what to include/remove is appreciated.

item.title: Birthdays For The Lonely
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes.

Page Type: SCP Article.

Elevator Pitch: The ghost of a 12 year old Caucasian boy who prepares and celebrates birthdays with people following the list of characteristics:

  • absence of both parents/abusive parent(s)
  • low self-esteem
  • not socially active
  • introverted
  • a victim of bullying
  • having little to no friends throughout life
  • no criminal record
  • never celebrated a proper birthday

If said person follows the list, a letter will appear inviting them to a party with directions to an isolated room. Once subject enters the room, in will be a party with a theme based on their interests. They also treat the boy as if he were a childhood friend. At the end of the party, he will give them a present, a physical object of what they wanted since they were younger. If someone interferes or the person ignores the invitation, the party will de-manifest and another apology letter will be sent the next day as well as said gift.

Central Narrative: Invitations were sent to multiple D-Classes in multiple sites the past week. With the foundation made aware of the situation, they decided to test the anomaly. They sent a D-Class to follow the directions which led them to a room where it is currently monitored. There, he celebrates with the SCP and left the room a few hours later with the gift in his hands. Then, they sent another with the purpose of interviewing the SCP. The questions listed are pretty generic, like what their name was, how did they obtain the ability, etc. But there are two questions I would like to highlight:

+ show block
– hide block

How he died.
His parents constantly fights which led to his father drinking. Because of that, he would abuse the both of them. On his 12th birthday, he made a wish that him and mother would escape someday. But then, the father came in and killed his son with a bottle before attempting to kill his wife as well before she called the police and he was arrested.

Why he celebrates birthdays.
He wants people to experience what he couldn't and make the world a better place. Also, to make new friends.

After the interview, the D-Class decided to dedicate the party to him and even offered him his own present. In which, the SCP accepts.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The idea and the narrative tied to it.

Additional Notes: None.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: (Seeking Greenlight) The Perfect Sleeper
item.description: A person is granted a wish for a good night's sleep
item.content:encoded:
A person is granted a wish for a good night's sleep with frustrating and dangerous, possibly humorous to some, consequences.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Drama

Page Layout: A series of incident and experiment logs.

Elevator Pitch: Through a series of accidental occurrences, mental and environmental manipulation the foundation slowly discovers the horrifying and for some readers humorous extent a human individual will go through in order to reach his desired target, a suitable bed. Definition of suitable is affected by many factors and perceptions.

Central Narrative: Incident logs detail a series of increasingly frustrating disappearances for seemingly innocuous reasons that tie into a series of experiments of increasing complexity and difficulty/danger. Including discovery of suspect as result of jail cells bring unable to hold a man convicted of a massive series of breaking and entering.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Starting with the discovery of the test subject and first incident logs it's immediately apparent that a perfect Houdini has been combined with massive property damage. No lock to complex no wall too strong no bridge to far to stop this man from sleeping. Attempts are made to use this power for unstoppable infiltration.

Additional Notes: While leaving room for imagination it's hinted the subject is suffering from the results of a "Monkeys Paw" wish that included a specified sleeping time. Exact wording is never discovered but it's hinted at including that the subject may be much older than he looks.

item.title: Rose-coloured glasses SCP
item.description: Thoughts?
item.content:encoded:
STAFF NOTICE: According to the Draft Forum Guidelines, please do not post your entire draft in the forums. Make a sandbox page, and keep your draft there. Please note that as this is the Ideas Critique forum, sandbox links should only be posted if a reviewer specifically asks to see a full draft.

If the only copy of your draft is in this post, you can recover the text by clicking the 'Last edited on [dd/mm/yyyy], [time] by [username] Show more' section found in lower right corner of this post. Then click 'Show revision' to find the full draft from before. Copy that full draft and paste it into your sandbox page. Please note that 'Show revision' does not change the post back.

item.title: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
item.description: A somewhat humanoid entity that is just mysterious
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Mystery

Elevator Pitch: A mysterious female being with some anomalys about her like an undefinable age and a bad forecast for the future of humankind.

Central Narrative: The main database entry acts as a brief introduction and explanation to the viewer, the main narrative really happens in the interviews with the SCP. The main point of the story is the entity's forecast of THEM, which are never rally explained and leave room for interpretation.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The entitys character. Especially in the interviews, the entity seems humorous and lively.

item.title: Criticism and Review
item.description: A 782 word draft about Copyright law and **totally normal humans**.
item.content:encoded:
Huzzah! I am finally ready to begin work on my first SCP!

I do not require green lights as I already have a +1 post on the main site (here)

Here it is:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/doggols-sandbox-ii

Under the tab 'Criticism and Review:**

My concerns:

  • Weirdness - Is it too hard to understand, can the mystery be solved?
  • Length - Is it too long or too short?
  • Story - Is the story behind it good enough?
  • Consistency - Is the anomaly consistent?

Fair warning, this is a Department of Misscomunications SCP, so It will be a bit confusing to readers. This is intentional.

spoilers, only click after reading
hide

Basically, a group of distinctly non-human individuals are messing with the Australian legal system. They've been using it to strike money out of people who break their laws copyright, and anyone who notices they're not human gets arrested for slander. These entities are incredibly tall, and leave a lot of mess wherever they go.

item.title: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness
item.description: Seeking greenlights and tips! Any tip will be appreciated
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Horror, Other: Discovery

Page Layout (Optional): The page will have some test logs, addendums and audio logs

Elevator Pitch: In Antarctica a dark portal to another dimension suddenly appears, after some scientists get lost in Antarctica the foundation started an investigation. That is when they found the dark warriors. Lead by a general corrupted by darkness and the darkness is literal darkness they want the world to be dark with no light source in sight. So the goal of the Dark army is: Destroy all light in any dimension that exists, They want darkness to stay in all dimensions since darkness makes them stronger and if they are at their strongest they can rule multiple dimensions and establish several civilizations. Sort of nations, kingdoms and or empires but on a large scale

Central Narrative: After the foundation finds the portal and the creatures a provisional site is built around the portal. The SCP includes three entities, the general, his soldiers (proxies) and the portal. The general commands his proxies or the proxies act on their own attacking any life-forms not "Embracing the dark". The general and his proxies are dark they don't have: skin, skeletons or even organs. the general is rarely seen his soldiers are mostly in our dimension and not in the "Dark" dimension. Both the soldiers and the general can corrupt biological life forms by killing them but the portal only provides a mode of transportation from dimension to dimension, when a human is killed a dark goo consumes them and turns them into a soldier but if the body is too damaged or did not die by the hands of the entities no transformation will happen. All dark entities have a severe case of Photophobia1 since light can hurt and melt them. Inside the portal there seems to be a world which had a SK-Class Dominance Shift Scenario where the entities took over earth in a crusade or conquest to end all life and rule over all dimensions but humans still survive in that dimension they are near extinction but they did survive. The foundation also found the general is building an army which could result in a BI Class Pre-Invasion Scenario. In which case Project L.B.L2 Is to be launched

+ Show more
- Show less

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The attention grabber here is the army. While it is obvious the army will attack the tense feeling of not knowing when and the size of the army will give interest to readers to continue reading.

Additional Notes: None

Footnotes
1. Fear of light
2. Project "Let there Be Light" Using mega flash lights known as M.F.L's to destroy the entities

item.title: SCP idea seeking greenlight/help!!!
item.description: I am seeking greenlight and critique to start on the SCP
item.content:encoded:
My idea is to make a twist on the traditional story of the monster under the bed. SCP-5334 will be a medium size creature with a elongated face. SCP-5334 appears to be cut in half but shows not signs of bleeding, SCP-5334 walks around on it two drills it has for arms. When SCP-5334 finds a bed/any type of sleeping thing elevated above the ground at least 30 cm it will attach its lower half, to the bottom of the bed, when the user falls into the 4th stage of sleep/the deepest sleep it will wake the user which we will now refer to as SCP-5334-1 and put SCP-5334-1 into Sleep paralysis then proceeding by using its drill like arms to make two 8 cm by 8cm holes in the lower chest. It will then proceed by eating the Heart and organs of SCP-5334-1 before proceeding to the next victim.

This is a small description of the scp, i hope you can get back to me with thing i can change or make better.

item.title: Dr. Ethan Millan
item.description: I want to create my first SCP, so I'm seeking greenlight and suggestion/help.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article, Tale

Genre (Optional): Drama/Emotional

Page Layout (Optional): Series of test logs and incident logs, and tales

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX, also known as Dr. Ethan Millan is a 26 years old caucasian male, a very talented researcher, who is from an alternative universe, and seemingly have an immunity to all anomalous effects (he doesn't have an immunity of bone breaking, just the memetic effects and some physical (he won't turn into something after touching an object, or get into a different reality)). He has a temporal immortality, meaning after death, he reanimates into the state of his arrival.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative: He was working on a time-machine, and as he was testing on a potato, he blew the mechanism up, taking himself into another reality. He landed into the middle of an O5 meeting, and after he was interviewed, it became clear that he was from a parallel universe. He was given amnestics to forget the O5 council, but it didn't work on him. After a few tests, it was suggested that he has immunity to all anomalous effects, and he was classified as an SCP subject. He was trying to create a way back to his world with the help of some foundation scientist, but it ended in faliure, as this world had different physics than his own. At this point, he requested to work as a researcher, which was accepted after a psychic evaluation. They were sure, that he won't give up the O5 council embers identities, as he has proven himself trustworthy, and he has immunity to all anomalous effects. After testing on a few Safe class SCPs he died in a contaiment breach. His body immediately disappeared, and after about 3 minutes, it reanimated in a brigth flash of light in the already compromised meetingroom, in the exact state as he arrived in to this universe. After this, he was reaccepted as a researcher, after giving him a briefing of things that happened to him, leaving out the classified bits. A few testing was made on his immortality. After a random interval of time, briefly between 15 seconds and 5 days, when he dies he reanimates at the same state as he arrived. Presumably because he channeled himself into this world's timestream.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: He can be used to explore some SCPs further, as he can use his immunity as an advantage (for example: memetic effects), and as he is "immortal" (not really, as he really dies when he dies, just reanimates in the exact location and state), he can be used after some falied test (for example: looking at 173 and expecting it not to move). Also, in tales, a dramatic story will happen: he will fall in love with his assigned researcher assistant (he needs someone to fill in the blanks after he dies). The main plot would be that what will happen with his relationship to others, after he "dies".

Additional Notes: SCP-XXXX was reclassified from Safe to Euclid class, after discovering his immortality. The meeting room at Site-xx was refurnished to be his personal arriving-chamber, and facility gurads were assigned to guard that location. He was assigned an assistant researcher to help him in his studies. Keter testing, and other testing which could resolve in his death, need a minimum of Level 4 clearance, and his personal approval. Requests for his self-termination can be accepted, if he has suffered unbearable psychological damage(he is still human).


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. Also, please note the Ideas Critique required reading wordcount limit; your post is about 145 words over. Please address this before seeking further feedback. ~Zyn

item.title: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]
item.description: First tale for my character, Dr. Olivia Blackburn.
item.content:encoded:
I've had this character in my mind for a while and have been working on a tale for her I would like to publish. Without explaining everything about her, she is a doctor of psychology who works (mostly) with low-threat humanoid SCPs, evaluating their mental states within containment and helping the Foundation better straddle the line between "this is cold but necessary" and "this is unreasonably cruel".
And because this is the Foundation, she has a minor anomalous ability. Extending from various points on people's bodies she can see strings of light no wider than a spider's thread extending, and these threads connect to other people based on familial relation. For example, siblings are all linked by threads extending from their inner left wrists. It's not a story with many practical implications, but in a workplace where knowing something you aren't supposed to can be extremely dangerous she feels very fortunate that she's kept her ability secret since childhood.
The main basis of this little tale is that I want to write about some of her observations and discoveries and the struggles that this knowledge comes with. On one hand a lot of the information she has could be very useful (if SCPs are related to each other, if ones assumed to be 'created' like the Little Misters have signs of parentage or families), but on the other hand knowing intimate family situations of Important People in the Foundation may not be something said people are much of a fan of. And then on a third hand, sometimes she just longs to be gossipy about it all.
It's not an extremely long story, only about 1500 words and mostly completed, but it expresses a lot of important things regarding Olivia's character that I might want to explore more in other ways.

Okay thanks for reading I sure hope I did this right!

item.title: The People Farmer (Seeking Greenlight and Feedback)
item.description: An entity that hunts and consumes people's personalities through dreams
item.content:encoded:
An entity that hunts and consumes people's personalities through dreams. This entity is connected to the origin of consciousness in humanity.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP article

Genre: Horror

Elevator Pitch:

An entity that appears to its victims in weird recurring dreams. Once it has chosen a victim, the entity wil subject them to personalized dreams designed to trick them into consenting to having their personality eaten. A victim who has gone through this process will loose memories, distinctive character traits and their overall sense of self. At the end of the process, the victim is left an empty shell (no memories or personality whatsoever). The entity has guided humanity to consciousness and organized society in order to harvest and eat our personalities.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative:

Through interviews with the victims and a dream journal the foundation pieces toghether who/what is the entity. At the end the foundation deduces that the entity feeds off personalities. We also discover that the victims are persuaded in their dreams to consent to being eaten through manipulation and deception. It seems that this entity is connected to the rise of consciousness in humans who has bred this trait in us like cattle to harvest. In order to study the anomaly, the foundation sends in an MTF specialized in advanced lucid dreaming. The MTF confronts the entity and through a cryptic conversation discovers the truth behind the entity. A deal is struck and the foundation manages to contain the entity by feeding it D-Class personel.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:

In this entry, what makes humanity special on planet earth is simply something this entity bred in humans through our dreams in order to hunt us for sport and sustenance. The entity itself also will have an unsettling atmosphere using its victims memories, fears and quirks in order to "eat" them. This SCP will also have description of strange dreamscapes once the MTF jumps in and contacts the entity directly. This will all be explained through interview logs since there is no way to look into people's dreams. The D-class sent to investigate first are thus unreliable narrators since they lack the proper lucid dreaming training and easily fall prey into the entity's deceptions.

Additional Notes:

Containment procedures:
-Finding eligible D-class and feeding them to the entity.
-Finding and dealing with any other victims outside of foundation control.

There will be an MTF involved in the article that specializes in "onironautics" (advanced lucid dreaming). A Specialist from MTF omicron rho could be a good fit.

The entity targets people who have led emptionally intense lives prefering those who have some kind or remorse since they are generally easier to manipulate in dreams.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Burning Rubber on the Mind
item.description: Parasitic fluke that digests rubber, forcing their hosts to eat rubber as well
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: What the summary says pretty much. We have an anomalous version of the Fasciola hepatica parasitic fluke. It worms its way into brains, and makes its hosts zombie-like in their pursuit of rubber. Plenty of rubber-related puns and jokes nestled in there (not super cheesy) for bonus containment points.

Central Narrative: Most of the narrative centers around the progression of the infection and symptom development. I also threw in an addendum about an outbreak at a NASCAR race which I think merges the humor of zombies at a raceway with the detached view of The Foundation in a humorous way.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: There are honestly relatively few biological SCPs that aren't extremely different from real world creatures. I understand why, because doing something that feels realistic is definitely more difficult and takes some of the fun out of the fantasy aspect. However, I think I could fill the lack of high-realism bio-SCPs if I work at it hard enough. I get big into the details of scientific stuff like this, so I think I can pull it off really well.

Additional Notes: I have already written up the entire page. I felt I could only elaborate on the realism aspect I am going for by doing the research and writing the whole thing to make sure it fit nicely. I know there are rules about posting the sandbox links unprompted, so if you are interested in seeing what I've done just ask.

item.title: SCP-5127
item.content:encoded:
The article is ok, though in many aspects could be improved, the foundation takes on a clinical tone in articles and idioms are usually not used in the tone, clinical tone is meant to be specific.

The rosy glasses are interesting but it is kind of like a regular anomalous object, the object just gives a rush of dopamine but it is just a pair of magical glasses that makes the wearer happy and doesn't really warrant an article, just contained on one of the security lockers with the key being held by a researcher and having an entry in the list of anomalous objects

I still believe that there's potential in here though, maybe add to it more? Take it more in a direction of your choosing.

item.title: [Seeking Greenlights]Traffic Disappearance
item.description: [thread locked, overflow]
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a phenomenon that occurs in the town of [REDACTED]. When a car passes an unmonitored road alone during the night, a green car of no number plate(SCP-XXXX-1) will manifest nearby and run into a car accident with it. Afterwards, SCP-XXXX-1, and whoever was in the car involved with the accident, will disappear.

Central Narrative: A journalist witnessed an SCP-XXXX event while researching for mysterious disappearance of citizens in traffic accidents. She collected information of every incident that might be related to SCP-XXXX and tried to come up with a scientific explanation to it. However, she was deceased in a car accident by SCP-XXXX a week later. Afterwards, the foundation was able to find a file in which the journalist recorded all of her foundings.

item.title: Concept for a new SCP, SCP-5258 "Vulnerability Blanket"
item.description: Yet another concept I have for an SCP
item.content:encoded:
Yet another concept I have for an SCP, this one being a physical object and not a living creature.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Page Layout: I plan on using the standard sort of SCP formatting that I've seen for most other articles use.

Elevator Pitch: I'd like to creature an object that's more or less the opposite of a security blanket, in that instead of hiding the wearer and making them feel protected, the blanket let's those not wearing it see the wearer's mental/physical wounds on their body, which can range from the locations of tumor to the presence of depression and/or other mental issues. After an extended period of use, the blanket would cause the wearer great distress, and if worn long enough, the subject could be prone to a manic breakdown.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I think this object could be a very interesting plot-point for future articles/tales, in that it can give those involved a better look at what they're dealing with when it comes to injuries and/or mental issues. I also just think it's a neat concept!

Additional Notes: All criticism is welcome here, and I'd love to hear people's thoughts on the subject! I also intend on the blanket having a sort of color coding affect, which I'll provide a chart for in the article, the colors themselves signaling which types of injuries/diseases are present on the wearer.

item.title: A Baby Toy
item.description: A baby toy with animals that play with children.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a spherical baby toy that has buttons on its surface that look like different animals. All of these buttons have been classified SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5, and it also has a roulette with a turtle as the arrow which is classified as SCP-XXXX-6. When SCP-XXXX is near the vicinity of a child under the age of 4 years SCP-XXXX instances will become animated this is its active phase, this will not happen if there is any individual above 4 years present in the room, nor will it happen if there are any video recorders present in the room. During SCP-XXXX active phase SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5 will proceed to play with the children(s), or play with each other though, SCP-XXXX-6 will not engage instead warning, or scolding the other SCP-XXXX instances if they would make acts that could harm the children(s).

Description of SCP-XXXX-1 : has the appearance of a red cat with a joyful smile.
Description of SCP-XXXX-2 : has the appearance of a brown bear in a laid down position.
Description of SCP-XXXX-3 : has the appearance of a purple bird with its wings being fully shown.
Description of SCP-XXXX-4 : has the appearance of a blue dog that is running happily.
Description of SCP-XXXX-5 : has the appearance of a white rabbit in the middle of jumping.
Description of SCP-XXXX-6 : has the appearance of a turtle laid down on their underside.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative:
The central narrative would be 1 audio recording with the SCP-XXXX instances, and 1 interview with them.
1 3 years old named David coming close to his birthday thus meeting SCP-XXXX requirement was put inside a room with SCP-XXXX, and an audio recorder was hidden on the side of a beanie bag.

David: What's happening to that toy?

SCP-XXXX-2:Well hello there buddy, want to come to play with us?

SCP-XXXX-3:Yeah you can join!!!…as long as you aren't rowdy as willy wagtail over there.

SCP-XXXX-4:Mind repeating that Miss Mary Quack?

SCP-XXXX-3:uhhh I didn't say anything.

David: What is this?

SCP-XXXX-4:Sorry don't mind us, so are you going to play or no?

David: Yeah! so what game are we playing?

Hook/Attention Grabber: It's a baby toy, and it has animals that are friendly to children

Additional Notes:. I need help to post a picture of SCP-XXXX,and 1 drawing of SCP-XXXX instances.I am sorry I call the animal buttons SCP-XXXX instances I didn't know what to call them.

item.title: Pelicans we, an SCP Article Proposal
item.description: My first SCP article idea.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a Stuffed Pelican that causes any individual who touches it, or is too close to begin to hallucinate that every living being is a pelican, as well as hearing the song "Pelicans we" by Cosmo Sheldrake at high volumes to the point that it hurts the ears and causes headaches. Sometimes also causes blurry vision. These symptoms last fifteen minutes after one leaves the area of effect and the song continues as if on repeat until the end of symptoms. This SCP is to be only handled by an individual wearing a sealed HAZMAT suit and is kept in a sealed glass container with a danger line on the floor marking the range of effect.

Central Narrative: This SCP is marked as Safe and/or Explained as it was discovered that it was manufactured in the 1960s and was held in possession of a prolific drug user who primarily used inhalable hallucinogens. The drugs and the anomalous energy caused by the drug use infused into the pelican causing it's effects. It has not been neutralized or destroyed, and research is going towards neutralization.

Hook/Attention Grabber: Not many "explained" SCPs are in effect currently (at least that I'm aware of, there are too many to be fully aware of all of them now) and it uses a historical period where drugs were common. There is still an anomalous nature that is common in SCP articles, so it works well with fitting in with the rest of them as well as being it's own thing.

Additional notes: The actual idea is inspired by the song itself, so I decided it should be included into the SCP itself. The song itself sounds like a drug trip, and is considered creepy by some of my friends, so it seemed like a good idea to state it as a hallucinogenic SCP. Then it made more sense to have a pelican involved as the actual object, and a stuffed animal seemed like a fair play. It's normally a rather insignificant and everyday object that is changed by it's anomalous properties. I'm also looking for critiques to make the proposal/other proposals in the future better, not just the greenlight to make it an article.

item.title: My First Idea
item.description: Idea for an SCP article involving a humanoid entity made of metal
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Humanoid entity with metallic skin
  • Reaction to people it encounters depends on their morality

Central Narrative: The SCP was once a normal human woman, possibly someone who worked for the Foundation, who was badly mutilated and killed in an accident that may or may not have involved another SCP. A few hours after her death, her body spontaneously reanimated and morphed into a large humanoid entity with skin that appeared to be made of metal. It was then taken into Foundation custody, where its abilities and personality became apparent during testing.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The SCP's gimmick is that it reacts differently to people it encounters depending on what it perceives their morality to be. Upon looking at a person it encounters, it can instantly gauge their morality, though the standards by which it defines good and evil aren't entirely understood. If it sees someone as not being evil, it will typically ignore them, but if it sees someone as evil, it experiences a dramatic increase in aggression, often attempting to kill them.

Additional Notes: If anyone has any feedback or suggestions as to how I could elaborate upon or improve the idea, I'd be glad to hear it. If the idea is too similar to an existing SCP, please let me know, since I might not be aware since there are so many SCPs.

item.title: The Moloch Mentality
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Grigori Karpin and Ralliston for their crit. Site-36 is basically community property, and the community ought to do more with it.

SCPs obliquely referenced:

SCP-089: Tophet by spikebrennan
SCP-475: Soap of a Pope by mlister and Tarage
SCP-4263: Gemini by Tanhony

Tales directly referenced:

Empire of Dirt (Part 3 by Djoric
GRANT REQUEST FOR UTILIZATION OF ELECTRO-THAUMIC COMPUTERS TO PERFORM EXORCISMS OF DEMONIC ENTITIES by GreenWolf

item.title: Fungal Colony
item.description: Aliens come in all shapes and sizes
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An alien fungus lands in an isolated town, infecting the surrounding plants as well as many of the mammals, birds, and a portion of the people turning the latter into violent cannibals. At first it seems like a standard zombie scenario but the fungus is in fact sentient and sapient and the violent ones were the result of an accident when they first arrived.

Central Narrative: The article begins by showing the SCP as a typical zombie pathogen then goes into some detail about their extraterrestrial origins before shifting to the Foundation discovering crude notes leading to the house of a serial killer with the implication that not all those infected are violent and those that are were the result of said killer being one of the first infected and his tendencies along with the fungi having never encountered intelligent life, creating a negative reaction. It then ends with their pleas to return to meteorite (which is actually an unconventional generation ship) so they can seek out a new home.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: A twist on the typical zombie outbreak along with some exploration about the unusual form alien life might take and how difficult it may be to communicate.

Additional Notes: May get slightly technical due to some real life parasite biology I'd like to integrate.

item.title: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?
item.description: Just an in-universe question.
item.content:encoded: I'm making a skip and it involves the Foundation Front company, Spicy Crust Pizza. I'm wondering if that company has an official email in-universe. If there is one, do you know what the name of it is?

item.title: (SEEKING GREENLIGHT) an existential philosophy
item.description: SCP philosophy
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes. got crit in IRC

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Philosophical

Elevator Pitch: A philosophy that makes SCP personnel realize that they are story characters

Central Narrative: The SCP foundation will discover that they are not real and suppress this information from not only the world but also themselves

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I'm going to have not only that personnel realize they are story characters but also they will realize the reader is there

Additional Notes: any critique or feedback welcome, I know this is a complex idea but i'm sure that I can run away with the idea

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
How is it even possible that SCPs are actually even allowed for publication? I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

Were the first couple thousand SCPs published before the publication became so strict?

I'm just not sure if people aren't creative enough, don't think it through enough, or if the moderators are too unwilling to give it a try.

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.description: A class taught on the "Philosophy of Existence"
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Psychological

Page Layout (Optional): Interview articles, "Sit-in Classes"

Elevator Pitch: Students who want college credit attend a class taught by an unusual professor.

Central Narrative: Letters from "South Euton University" are sent out to high school and college students who want college credit. The class being taught is labeled "Philosophy of Existence," and is taught by Professor Phillip Oliver Zofia. The classroom has an unusual setup, with strange posters and maps that have countries improperly labeled. Above the whiteboard is a large poster, upon which is written, "Cogito, Ergo Sum." The professor himself is a man described as "gaunt," "faint," even "ethereal."

+ show block
– hide block

The class begins with the professor providing the students with an initial quiz. The questions are very easy (2+2=?), but the students fail when they provide standard answers. The professor then proceeds to teach the class, telling them blatantly incorrect answers, and then testing them on the incorrect knowledge. Those that refuse to answer "properly" are given personal instruction by Dr. Zofia. The sessions apparently take half an hour, though one student describes it feeling as though she was in there for days (vague as to whether she was or not). Invariably, the students start giving him the answers he wants, whether through exasperation or actually starting to buy into his teaching. They become increasingly suggestible as this goes on. Simultaneously, Dr. Zofia grows stronger, fuller, more substantial.

The final test consists of a piece of paper, asking, "Cogito, ergo sum. Concur?" There is also a line where the students write their names, which they invariably do. At the conclusion of the test, the students disappear, while Dr. Zofia seems to become completely rejuvenated. The SCP agent monitoring this situation (implied that Zofia is amused by their presence and is toying with them) offers them a letter and an invitation to join them next semester, and then is ushered out.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Aside from the oddities of the classroom, the central question, "I think, therefore I am," is explored. If one stops thinking, do they really exist? And if one's thoughts supercede another's, does that not make them more you, than you? This is also meant to be mild commentary on the education system caring more about correct answers than understanding material.

Additional Notes:

item.title: SCP Idea - A living Zebra
item.description: A horse that will survive, somehow
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: It's a zebra that doesn't die. It doesn't have supernatural resilience, but circumstance always works out in such a way that it survives. If a roof collapses, the portion of roof over the zebra will turn out to be still standing.

Central Narrative: The foundation realizes it's probably a bad idea to fuck with something when it doesn't have to
-The concept is introduced ("lucky zebra"),
-A list of relevant extraordinary circumstances is given
-The last example is enumerated upon:
-Toxins are administered to the horse
-The test is aborted when it almost dies
-It is concluded the horse "accepted" the toxins because we would save it
-Dr.McGuffin proposes injecting it and not saving it
-Because it's just a Zebra, the only way it could survive is by something preventing the staff from injecting toxins
-Testing is suspended

Hook/Attention-Grabber: It's a probability warper that warps around the idea of being a normal zebra. Although it poses no risk of breaching containment, because it's a normal zebra, containment will be breached if that it what's necessary to keep it alive.

Additional Notes: It's written in the vein of many older SCPs: very brief, and more "weird with dangerous implications" than scary

item.title: SCP Cosplay suggestions
item.content:encoded:
So in my country there is no Halloween but a holiday later in the year called Yom Kipur (יום כיפור)
and at that holiday you dress up as anything you want and generally celebrate in school, work, parties etc… So for this year i wanted to cosplay as a humanoid SCP of course but I don't know which one. Send me suggestions and I will pick the most interesting ones. Thanks!

item.title: They Lie In The Shadows (Seeking Greenlight)
item.description: A routine mission turns into a life or death escape attempt
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Layout: SCP article

Genre: Horror

Page Layout: The first part is a description of the actual SCP creatures. Then it transitions to the audio logs from the mission made by its commander.

Elevator pitch: A city is revealed to be overrun by tall and abnormally skinny creatures with long fingers, an almost circular head, and an abnormally large mouth. The creatures also originated from some kind of fungus growth, which results in their outer appearance being unusual bumpy and allows them to reproduce at an astonishing speed. They can climb up and down 90-degree surfaces with ease and can distort their bodies in ways that shouldn’t be possible. The creatures appear to be blind but possess some kind of echolocation as they will make a clicking noise when on the hunt. The creatures seem almost immune to bullets although enough bullets will kill them. The creatures do have one major weakness though, light. It appears that any kind of light burns the creature’s skin and they will always try to avoid it.

Central Narrative: The foundation gets word of multiple disappearances along with reported sightings of humanoid figures in [redacted], so just to be safe, they check it out. But soon discover that the city has been destroyed by an unknown entity or entities. After this discovery, a task force is sent to find out what happened, but the mission goes from a simple search and report, to a living nightmare, as the deeper they go the more unsettling it gets. Soon many on the task force have feelings of being watched and see shapes in the corner of their eyes. Yet they are told to continue and ignore these fears. That is until it turns to night and these creatures come out of the shadows. The whole operation then turns into a life or death escape attempt.

Hook/Attention Grabber: The hook is the Task force’s journey throughout the city that later turns into a life or death escape attempt

Additional Notes: If you have any critiques or ideas for this I would really like to know. whether you think it's bad or good I don't really care I just want to know what people think.

item.title: The Power of The Atom -revised
item.description: Multidimensional Facility with a powerful entity trapped inside
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Mystery, Horror, Drama

Elevator pitch and central narrative
Elevator pitch and central narrative

Elevator Pitch: Foundation operatives discover a distress signal similar to that of Foundation Site-17, however Site-17 is not anywhere near the distress signal's origination. A MTF unit is deployed at origination point to discover a massive sinkhole going approximately 300 meters into the ground. The MTF repel down the shaft to find it breaches the roof of an identical reconstruction of Site-17.

The MTF unit discover dozens of burned corpses located around the facility, however no SCP items contained at that site can be found. Data logs found in a computer in the site talk about some sort of nuclear apocalypse, the last day recorded being June 15th, 2077. The unit make their way toward the generator room of the facility in an attempt to restore power when they find a child sitting in one of the hallways. The MTF ask the child what happened and why he was here. The child described: "My daddy drove us here… he is gone now. The monster sucked the life out of him. Are you here to kill the monster? I trapped it, let me show you where!" The MTF follow the child to the generator room.

When they reach the room, they find a high tech electromagnetic fence surrounding the generators and a large humanoid lifeform trapped on the fence. One of the MTF decided to turn off the fence with the control panel on it. The entity fell to the floor with a bang. It rose from the ground and suddenly a large explosion came from the entity causing everything within the room to be destroyed, but not the entity.

Central Narrative: The lifeform is the reason the facility exists. The facility is from another reality and somehow got to this reality. In another reality, a nuclear war occurred causing most of humanity to be wiped out, except those in the facility. They were supposed to escape but the creature stopped them. The creature seems to be able to cause great damage to areas around it. In interviews, the creature claims to be the one that caused the nuclear disaster in the alternate reality.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The creature is a reality bender of immense proportions, with the capability to cause atomic destruction with a thought. It could also be used as a weapon against other groups… or SCPs.

Additional Notes:

The Facility and the entity will be split into 2 different SCPs.

I am planning to add many interviews, exploration logs, and crosstests with other SCPs.

item.title: Social Suicide: SCP Idea
item.description: An object that erases anyone who comes in contact with it
item.content:encoded:
An object that erases anyone who comes in contact with it from the memories of anybody that has ever met them.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: No

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): N/A (Yet)

Elevator Pitch: If you come in contact with the object, all memories of you will be erased from everyone you ever met.

Central Narrative: The central narrative focuses on 4 instances of the use of the objects and how this affected the lives of the victims.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: After testing, the foundation finds files on high profile personnel that nobody has ever heard of meaning that the object has been found before and been used as a "weapon" against government officials, civilians, and even other SCPs in the Foundation.

Additional Notes: I'm playing around with the idea that the object could be used for taking high priority persons and SCPs since it would be a quick an easy way to make someone disappear without having to give amnestics to a large number of people. I'm also thinking of making the object a small eraser.
